prompt_md: A multi-day AI research assistant must reference a large, frequently-updated policy library and also honor a few non-negotiable constraints (e.g. 'exclude EU-region records') that were stated at the very start of a session. Long sessions previously violated those early constraints after older turns were compressed. **What is the best way to keep the constraints reliably in context, and why?**

decision_choices: Pin the non-negotiable constraints as protected facts carried verbatim every call, and use retrieval for the policy library
Switch to the largest available context window and re-send the entire transcript each call
Use a pure sliding window of the last 15 turns and nothing else
Summarize everything, including the constraints, into a single running summary
reason_choices: Because pinned facts bypass lossy summarization while retrieval keeps the large, changing library out of the window until needed
Because a larger window guarantees the model attends equally to all positions in the context
Because recent turns are the only context that ever matters for correctness
Because compressing the constraints into the summary makes them more durable over time
